page: Amazon Web Services m8azn.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R05 processor operating at a high clock speed of 5.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, this instance allocates 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU includes 32 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache, alongside advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier (top 10%) for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 22646.78 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 1055.41 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. In database and web serving tests, the instance achieved 332.74 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 158.23 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. Large language model inference benchmarks show top-tier prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large models, though text generation for small models falls into the average category.

From a resource density perspective, the instance offers high-frequency compute capabilities and fast DDR5 memory, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high single-thread performance without the overhead of a GPU. However, tradeoffs include the complete absence of local NVMe storage, meaning all storage operations rely on network-attached EBS volumes. Additionally, network baseline bandwidth is capped at 200 Gbps, and no complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

This instance configuration is suited for high-throughput database operations, software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving. It is also applicable for CPU-based LLM inference tasks, particularly prompt processing and large-model text generation, as well as memory-intensive general-purpose enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R05 physical cores running at 5.0 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 memory. Utilizing the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance delivers top-tier CPU and memory performance, as evidenced by top 10% rankings in stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark benchmarks. It achieves a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps but lacks local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Qualitatively, the instance provides high resource density for compute-heavy and memory-intensive tasks without GPU overhead. It is highly effective for database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based large language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 dedicated AMD EPYC cores at 5.0 GHz, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier CPU and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.24xlarge features 96 AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 200 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier compute performance.
tagline: A high-frequency, general-purpose instance combining 96 dedicated AMD cores with fast DDR5 memory and high-speed network performance.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R05 physical cores operating at 5.0 GHz.
384.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory (4.0 GB per core).
Top-tier single-core, multi-core, and database benchmark performance.
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration.
No local storage, requiring network-attached EBS volumes.
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference.
